---
title: "About"
image: profile.jpg
about:
  template: solana
  image-width: 10em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yazid-janati-414a4a1b7/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/yazidjanati

---

I am a PhD candidate in statistics at Télécom SudParis - Institut Polytechnique de Paris, advised by [Sylvain Le Corff](https://sylvainlc.github.io) and [Yohan Petetin](http://www-public.imtbs-tsp.eu/~petetin/). 

I am mainly interested in Monte Carlo methods (importance sampling, sequential monte carlo) and these days i'm focusing on their synergy with variational inference. My current focus is driven by the following questions: 

- How to break the curse of dimensionality when optimizing the inclusive Kullback-Leibler divergence and $\alpha$ divergence?
